July in
Fucecchio

Among the reeds of the Padule di Fucecchio, Tuscany

MonthJuly 2026
Volunteers18 of you
BaseFucecchio (FI)
Getting aroundOn foot & by train
Trail AngelsWelcome
Why we're here

A month in the
heart of Tuscany.

For the July edition, 18 volunteers from across Europe come together in Fucecchio. You'll explore the marsh, meet locals, grow some skills, and look out for each other along the way.

Live together, well

Two houses, one group. You will share the cooking and make the space feel like home.

Learn from each other

A skill exchange, a secret friend, a workshop on what comes next. But remember, the best part is always the people.

Explore Tuscany

Pisa, Lucca and Florence are all a short train ride away. Weekends are yours to roam around.

Trail AngelsWhere you'll live
Fucecchio · Padule di Fucecchio

A town beside the
largest marsh in Italy

Fucecchio sits on the Arno, halfway between Florence, Pisa and Lucca. Just beside it lies the Padule di Fucecchio — the largest inland marsh in Italy, alive with herons, storks and dragonflies. It's also the land where Leonardo da Vinci's mother was born.

🪺 Wetland nature reserve 🚆 30–50 min to 3 great cities ☀️ Long Tuscan summers
Trail AngelsYour homes
Where you'll stay

Two houses, one group

12volunteers
Main house

Ostello Ponte dè Medici

The bigger of the two — twelve of you here. A hostel right in Fucecchio.

6volunteers
Second house

Depandance Villa Ruben

A smaller, cosier base for six. Around 30 minutes away by walking.

Trail AngelsThe programme
European Solidarity Corps

What is ESC?

An EU programme that lets young people volunteer abroad for causes they care about — fully funded. You’re part of it right now — here’s what that really means.

🤝

Solidarity, not tourism

You give your time and energy to a place and its people — and grow while doing it.

🌱

Learning by doing

No experience needed. You pick up languages, skills and confidence through real work.

🇪🇺

A European family

You join thousands of young people across the continent — friends and doors that last.

18–30

For young people

Open to anyone aged 18 to 30 in a participating country.

€0

Fully funded

Travel, housing, food and pocket money are all covered.

2–12

Months abroad

Projects run from a couple of weeks to a full year.

35+

Countries

Across Europe and beyond, on every kind of cause.

✓ A Youthpass certificate ✓ Insurance & full support ✓ Language learning help ✓ A network across Europe
Trail AngelsA little kindness game
Introducing · Secret Friend

Someone is quietly rooting for you

?
Hover to reveal
🤫

It's a secret!

You'll receive a name at the start of the month — and keep it hidden until the very last day.

i

Receive a name

At the start, everyone receives the name of another volunteer. That person is now your secret friend.

ii

Small, kind gestures

Throughout the month, do little anonymous things for them — a note, a coffee, a small surprise. Stay undercover.

iii

The big reveal

On the last night, everyone guesses — then we reveal who had who. Expect plenty of laughs.

Trail AngelsLearn from each other
Skill exchange day

Everyone teaches one thing

One day this month, each of you brings a skill — big or small — and shares it with the group.

1

Pick your skill

Anything you can show in 20 minutes. A recipe, a dance, a card trick, a few words of your language — it all counts.

cookingmusiclanguage
2

Share it simply

Set up a little corner, gather whoever's curious, and teach. Hands-on beats slideshows every time.

photographyyogadrawing
3

Leave with more

By the end of the day everyone has learnt something new — and taught something only they could.

jugglingcodingcrafts
✍️
Take a moment to think about what you can share — then write it down in the “Skill exchange” section of our Padlet.
Trail AngelsExplore Tuscany
Places around you

Three cities, one train line

~40 min by train

Pisa

The leaning tower, of course — but also a lively student city on the Arno, with riverside walks and great food.

~45 min by train

Lucca

A beautiful town, amazing for cycling around. Castle walls and towers with trees on them, and our CNV day is later this month.

~50 min by train

Florence

The Renaissance capital. The Duomo, the Uffizi, sunset from Piazzale Michelangelo. Worth a whole free weekend.

Trail AngelsA special day
The Lucca day

A day of connection
in Lucca

One day we all take the train to Lucca together. A day to slow down, communicate better, see a beautiful city, and look ahead to what's possible after this.

🗣️

CNV — Nonviolent Communication

A gentle workshop on listening and speaking with empathy — a skill you'll carry far beyond this project.

🚶

A city excursion with Rebecca

Rebecca will guide you through Lucca — its walls, towers and hidden corners — so you really get to know the city.

🇪🇺

Opportunities in the EU

A workshop on what comes next — ESC, Erasmus+ and other ways to keep volunteering, studying and travelling across Europe.

Trail AngelsStay safe
In an emergency

Keep this page handy

General emergency number (EU) 112

One number across all of Europe — police, ambulance and fire. Free to call from any phone.

Project contact
Javi
+34 681 22 43 12

If something happens

1
Call 112 first if it's urgent.
2
Then inform one of the project contacts as soon as you can.
3
You're never alone in this — reach out, day or night.
Trail AngelsYou matter here
The Safe Space form

This is a safe space.

The form is here to support your safety, well-being and overall experience during the project. If something doesn't feel right — or you simply want to share — you're always welcome to express yourself.

Scan to open
The Safe Space form ↗
💬

Speak openly

Honestly, and in your own way. There's no wrong way to share how you feel.

🕊️

Stay anonymous if you prefer

You can choose to leave your name out entirely. It's completely up to you.

🤝

Taken seriously, always

Every message is treated with care, respect and full confidentiality.

2

What kind of atmosphere do you hope for in the group?

3

4

What can others expect from you?

Benvenuti a
Fucecchio.

In bocca al lupo — the Trail Angels team
